title: dryopteris parksii
intro: Dryopteris parksii, also known as Parks' oak fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of fern that is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal and health-promoting properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The dried rhizomes and leaves of the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
